perturbation.original_text: BATTELLE and its representative(s) shall maintain the identified Proprietary Information in confidence for a period of three (3) years from the effective date of this Agreement. During this period, BATTELLE shall not divulge such information to any third party or use such information for any purpose other than review and evaluation without the prior written consent of ADC. BATTELLE shall treat such information with the same degree of care as it accords to its own proprietary information.
perturbation.changed_text: BATTELLE and its representative(s) shall maintain the identified Proprietary Information in confidence for a period of one (1) year from the effective date of this Agreement. During this period, BATTELLE shall not divulge such information to any third party or use such information for any purpose other than review and evaluation without the prior written consent of ADC. BATTELLE shall treat such information with the same degree of care as it accords to its own proprietary information.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by reducing the confidentiality period for Proprietary Information from three (3) years to one (1) year in Section 3. This contradicts the statement in Section 6 that "Article 3 shall survive termination," which implies that the original three-year confidentiality obligation should remain in effect even after the agreement terminates after one year. This change creates uncertainty about the actual duration of confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Washington and any action brought to enforce any provision or obligation hereunder shall be brought in a court of competent jurisdiction in the State of Washington. The prevailing party in any such proceeding shall be entitled to receive from the other party all reasonable attorneys' fees incurred by such prevailing party and all costs reasonably incurred in connection therewith. The term of this Agreement shall be one (1) year, or as extended by written modification. Article 3 shall survive termination. The effective date of this Agreement shall be determined by the date affixed hereto by the party last signing this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated a three-year confidentiality period in Article 3. The changed text reduces this period to one year. The contradicted text states that 'Article 3 shall survive termination', but the agreement now terminates after one year, contradicting the survival of the original three-year confidentiality obligation. This directly contradicts the survival clause and creates uncertainty regarding the actual confidentiality period. Therefore, a strong contradiction exists.

perturbation.original_text: All disclosures of "Proprietary Information" will be in writing and marked "PROPRIETARY' or equivalent words by ADC at the time such writings are first furnished to BATTELLE.
perturbation.changed_text: All disclosures of "Proprietary Information" will be in writing but may or may not be marked "PROPRIETARY' or equivalent words by ADC at the time such writings are first furnished to BATTELLE.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by making the labeling of "Proprietary Information" as "PROPRIETARY" optional rather than mandatory in Section 2. This creates uncertainty because other sections of the agreement rely on the clear identification of Proprietary Information to enforce confidentiality obligations. If information is not clearly marked, it becomes difficult to determine what is subject to the agreement's protections, undermining the entire NDA's purpose. Section 3 now becomes problematic due to this in-text contradiction, due to the fact that it's not a must for the proprietary information to be marked.
perturbation.contradicted_text: BATTELLE and its representative(s) shall maintain the identified Proprietary Information in confidence for a period of three (3) years from the effective date of this Agreement. During this period, BATTELLE shall not divulge such information to any third party or use such information for any purpose other than review and evaluation without the prior written consent of ADC. BATTELLE shall treat such information with the same degree of care as it accords to its own proprietary information.
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated that 'Proprietary Information' be marked as such. The changed text makes this marking optional. The contradicted text requires 'identified Proprietary Information' to be kept confidential. By making the marking optional, it creates a contradiction because it is no longer clear what qualifies as 'identified Proprietary Information,' thus creating a strong in-text contradiction. The changed text introduces uncertainty and thus conflicts with the contradicted text. This adheres to the definition of Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall be one (1) year, or as extended by written modification.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall be five (5) year, or as extended by written modification.
perturbation.explanation: This change extends the initial term of the agreement from one (1) year to five (5) years in Section 6. This contradicts the provision in Section 3 that requires BATTELLE to maintain Proprietary Information in confidence for a period of only three (3) years. While Article 3 shall survive termination, the time doesn't match the time in which agreement is valid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: BATTELLE and its representative(s) shall maintain the identified Proprietary Information in confidence for a period of three (3) years from the effective date of this Agreement. During this period, BATTELLE shall not divulge such information to any third party or use such information for any purpose other than review and evaluation without the prior written consent of ADC. BATTELLE shall treat such information with the same degree of care as it accords to its own proprietary information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the agreement term from one year to five years. This contradicts the contradicted text, which states that BATTELLE must maintain proprietary information in confidence for only three years. The extended agreement term now exceeds the confidentiality period, creating a direct conflict as the agreement is valid for longer than the confidentiality clause covers, even though the confidentiality clause survives termination.

perturbation.original_text: Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder shall remain the property of ADC. No license under any patent, copyright, trademark or trade secret is granted or implied.
perturbation.changed_text: Proprietary Information disclosed hereunder shall become the joint property of ADC and BATTELLE. No license under any patent, copyright, trademark or trade secret is granted or implied.
perturbation.explanation: This change modifies Section 5 to state that Proprietary Information becomes the *joint* property of ADC and BATTELLE, instead of remaining solely the property of ADC. This directly contradicts the fundamental nature of a non-disclosure agreement, where the disclosing party (ADC) retains ownership. Now the ADC no longer have ownership of the data, going against the definition of the NDA agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Proprietary Information" is confidential and proprietary information relating to: 1) Protection of employee medical records relative to drug testing results, 2) Protection of medical records relative to infectious disease testing, 3) Protection of genetic testing records, and 4) Biometric identification of patients and results in the fields of pharmacy, blood banking, radiology and laboratory specimens; and related projects.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters Section 5 to state that Proprietary Information becomes the *joint* property of ADC and BATTELLE, instead of remaining solely the property of ADC. The contradicted text defines 'Proprietary Information' as relating to specific confidential information, implying ADC's control and protection of this information. By making it joint property, the changed text contradicts the inherent confidentiality and proprietary nature described in the contradicted text because ADC no longer has sole control over their own data, leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: BATTELLE shall treat such information with the same degree of care as it accords to its own proprietary information.
perturbation.changed_text: BATTELLE shall treat such information with a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.explanation: This change modifies Section 3, relaxing BATTELLE's obligation to protect ADC's Proprietary Information. Originally, BATTELLE was required to treat ADC's information with the same degree of care as its own proprietary information. The modified text only requires a "reasonable" degree of care. What reasonable constitutes could be argued and can diminish the legal obligation that BATTELLE has. This contradicts section 1 of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Proprietary Information" is confidential and proprietary information relating to: 1) Protection of employee medical records relative to drug testing results, 2) Protection of medical records relative to infectious disease testing, 3) Protection of genetic testing records, and 4) Biometric identification of patients and results in the fields of pharmacy, blood banking, radiology and laboratory specimens; and related projects.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies BATTELLE's obligation to protect ADC's Proprietary Information. The original text required BATTELLE to treat ADC's information with the same degree of care as its own, while the changed text only requires a "reasonable" degree of care. This introduces ambiguity and potentially diminishes BATTELLE's legal obligation. This change contradicts the definition of 'Proprietary Information' outlined in the contradicted text, which implies a high degree of protection for sensitive employee medical records, genetic testing records, and biometric data, suggesting 'reasonable care' may be insufficient. Therefore, the modified text creates a direct conflict and contradiction within the document as it relates to the level of security Battelle needs to provide based on the definition of Proprietary Information.

perturbation.original_text: It is understood by the parties that this obligation of confidentiality shall not apply to information which: 1. is published or becomes published or otherwise becomes generally available to the public through no breach of this Agreement by BATTELLE; or 2. BATTELLE can show was properly in its possession prior to receipt of the disclosure from ADC; or 3. is independently developed by BATTELLE staff not having access to ADC Proprietary Information as demonstrated by competent documentary evidence; or 4. becomes available to BATTELLE from an independent source without breach of agreement or violation of law; or <PAGE> 5. is required to be disclosed pursuant to proper governmental or judicial process, provided that notice of such process is promptly provided to ADC in order that ADC may have every reasonable opportunity to intervene in such process to contest such disclosure
perturbation.changed_text: It is understood by the parties that this obligation of confidentiality shall not apply to information which: 1. is published or becomes published or otherwise becomes generally available to the public through no breach of this Agreement by BATTELLE; or 2. BATTELLE can show was properly in its possession prior to receipt of the disclosure from ADC; or 3. is independently developed by BATTELLE staff not having access to ADC Proprietary Information as demonstrated by competent documentary evidence; or 4. becomes available to BATTELLE from an independent source without breach of agreement or violation of law; or <PAGE> 5. is required to be disclosed pursuant to proper governmental or judicial process
perturbation.explanation: The change in section 4, removes the provision that requires BATTELLE to promptly provide notice to ADC when Proprietary Information is required to be disclosed pursuant to governmental or judicial process. This contradicts the purpose of section 1, which aims to protect Proprietary Information. Without notification, ADC loses the opportunity to intervene and contest disclosure, undermining its ability to protect the confidential information that is the subject of this Non-Disclosure Agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: "Proprietary Information" is confidential and proprietary information relating to: 1) Protection of employee medical records relative to drug testing results, 2) Protection of medical records relative to infectious disease testing, 3) Protection of genetic testing records, and 4) Biometric identification of patients and results in the fields of pharmacy, blood banking, radiology and laboratory specimens; and related projects.
perturbation.justification: The original text provided ADC with an opportunity to intervene in any governmental or judicial process that would require disclosure of Proprietary Information. The changed text removes this notification requirement. The contradicted text defines 'Proprietary Information' which the original text sought to protect through notification, now lost in the changed text. This directly weakens the protection of Proprietary Information, creating a contradiction.
